name: Get-AutopilotDiagnosticsCommunity
description: A PowerShell diagnostic script for analyzing Windows Autopilot deployments. Provides comprehensive details about Autopilot profile settings, policies, apps, and certificate profiles tracked via Enrollment Status Page, with support for local PC analysis and captured log files.
keywords: why is autopilot taking so long
author: Andrew Taylor

securityCheck.aiSummary: The code largely implements a broad backup/restore workflow that copies extensive user data to OneDrive locations. While this can be legitimate in managed environments, it triggers significant data exfiltration/privacy concerns due to inclusion of highly sensitive data (PSTs, emails, RoamCache) and involves invisible execution wrappers that can obscure activity. No remote script downloads, credential harvesting, or hardcoded secrets observed. Recommend data minimization, encryption, access controls, user notification, signing/auditing of invisible executables, and alignment with organizational data governance policies.

noRemoteExecution.reason: IEX with WebClient download in Powershell Scripts/AVD/avd-box-config-generic.ps1 ([lines: 224](https://github.com/andrew-s-taylor/public/blob/main/Powershell Scripts/AVD/avd-box-config-generic.ps1#L224))

noDataExfiltration.reason: Large-scale backup of user data is performed by copying numerous directories (Documents, Music, Signatures, RoamCache, Favourites, Chrome bookmarks, PSTs, Word/Excel/UI templates, Sticky Notes, Start Menu items, etc.) to OneDrive and OneDriveCommercial. This constitutes broad data exfiltration to cloud storage. While intended as backup, it risks exposing highly sensitive data (e.g., PST/Outlook data, RoamCache, email signatures) and raises privacy/data governance concerns. Recommend data minimization, encryption at rest/in transit, strict access controls, and explicit user consent policies.
